mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over rolling 3-year windows, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in over 60 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with interaction term coefficient statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a decline in the index of industrial production signals supply-side contraction that outweighs rate hike benefits, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings above 2.5 percent trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 65 percent of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields a negative or zero Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery phase, accelerating real GDP growth alongside rising personal consumption expenditures validates economic momentum, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 and RSAFS growth during recovery phases fails to generate positive 20-day SPX returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing output and business investment, directly supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in over 60 percent of observations across all market regimes.
